subject: Social Science (CBSE - History & Civics)
chapter: Social and Political Life – II: Understanding Media

activities_projects.title: Critical Media Analysis Report
activities_projects.objective: Analyze media bias and framing in depth
materials: Newspaper, online news portals, or TV news clips
steps: Select one national or international event reported by at least three sources
Compare language, framing, images, and placement of news
Write a detailed report analyzing bias, perspective, and omissions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop advanced media literacy and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, clarity, and critical thinking (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigative Project: Social Media Verification
activities_projects.objective: Learn to verify information and identify misinformation online
steps: Select a trending news story or viral post on social media
Investigate its authenticity using multiple credible sources
Prepare a report detailing verification steps, sources used, and conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students acquire skills to critically assess online information
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, methodology, and presentation of findings (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media Ethics Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand ethical dilemmas faced by media professionals
materials: Articles or case studies on media controversies
steps: Select one real-life case involving media ethics (e.g., sensationalism, invasion of privacy)
Analyze the ethical challenges and decisions made
Propose alternative solutions and justify them
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical reasoning and ethical awareness in media
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, depth of analysis, and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design a Media Literacy Workshop
activities_projects.objective: Teach peers how to consume media responsibly
materials: Chart paper, presentation software, or posters
steps: Plan a workshop with 3–4 activities teaching verification of news, detecting bias, and responsible consumption
Prepare instructional materials, examples, and exercises
Conduct a mini-session for classmates or record a video tutorial
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop leadership, communication, and media literacy skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Effectiveness, creativity, and engagement of workshop (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Advertisement Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how media uses persuasion and influence
steps: Select two advertisements promoting the same product or idea
Compare techniques used (emotional appeal, repetition, slogans, visuals)
Write a critical reflection on effectiveness and ethical considerations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and understanding of media influence
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison, critical reflection, and examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role-play: Media and Democracy
activities_projects.objective: Explore the role of media in shaping public opinion and democracy
steps: Assign roles: journalists, editors, politicians, public citizens
Simulate a scenario involving election coverage or public policy debate
Discuss the impact of reporting, misinformation, and accountability
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the democratic function and responsibilities of media
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation, understanding of roles, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigate Fake News Networks
activities_projects.objective: Understand how misinformation spreads and its impact
steps: Research one case where misinformation went viral online
Trace sources, intermediaries, and public response
Present a visual or written report explaining the spread and consequences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to critically analyze information flow and societal impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality, clarity, and insight (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Create a Multimedia Awareness Campaign
activities_projects.objective: Promote responsible media consumption using digital tools
steps: Design a campaign that educates peers on detecting fake news, verifying sources, and avoiding misinformation
Include videos, posters, and interactive elements
Share with the class or online group
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance media literacy, digital skills, and communication abilities
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, accuracy, and effectiveness of campaign (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigative Journalism Mini-Project
steps: Identify a local issue or event worth reporting
Collect information through interviews, observation, and research
Write a comprehensive news report including facts, context, and multiple perspectives
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop investigative, reporting, and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality, accuracy, and depth of reporting (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media Impact Research Paper
activities_projects.objective: Study the effect of media on society and behavior
steps: Select a topic (e.g., social media influence on teenagers, TV news on public opinion)
Conduct research using multiple credible sources
Write a 2–3 page report with findings, analysis, and conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn advanced research, analytical, and writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, clarity of analysis, and presentation (10 marks)
